query: Nissan warranty coverage details for powertrain and battery in Japan

request_params_without_api_key.q: Nissan warranty coverage details for powertrain and battery in Japan

search_parameters.q: Nissan warranty coverage details for powertrain and battery in Japan

text_blocks.snippet: In Japan, Nissan's factory warranty explicitly separates coverage periods into General Warranty (一般保証) and Special Warranty (特別保証), with specific, extended terms assigned to electric vehicles (EVs) and e-POWER hybrid models.
Nissan categories powertrain components under its Special Warranty (特別保証).

list.snippet: Coverage Period: 5 years or 100,000 kilometers, whichever occurs first.
Covered Components: The engine, transmission, transaxle, drivetrain, and related electronic control modules.

e-POWER Models: For e-POWER hybrid vehicles (like the Note or Serena), the e-POWER system components—including the generator and electric motor—are fully protected under this 5-year/100,000 km powertrain specification.

text_blocks.snippet: Pure electric vehicles (EVs) like the Nissan Leaf or Ariya feature dedicated factory protection covering both manufacturing defects and capacity degradation:

table: Covers repairs or replacements due to physical materials or factory workmanship flaws.
Guarantees the lithium-ion battery will not drop below 9 segments (out of 12) on the in-vehicle segment gauge. Nissan repairs or replaces the pack if it falls below this minimum threshold.

detailed.snippet: Covers repairs or replacements due to physical materials or factory workmanship flaws.
Guarantees the lithium-ion battery will not drop below 9 segments (out of 12) on the in-vehicle segment gauge. Nissan repairs or replaces the pack if it falls below this minimum threshold.

formatted.protection_details: Covers repairs or replacements due to physical materials or factory workmanship flaws.
Guarantees the lithium-ion battery will not drop below 9 segments (out of 12) on the in-vehicle segment gauge. Nissan repairs or replaces the pack if it falls below this minimum threshold.

text_blocks.snippet: Note: For e-POWER vehicles, the smaller high-voltage traction battery is typically backed by the standard 5-year or 100,000 km special hybrid-component breakdown criteria.

The standard lead-acid auxiliary 12V battery used to boot vehicle electronics is excluded from the main EV and special powertrain plans. It is handled separately via individual battery manufacturer policies or standard 1-to-3 year general maintenance schedules based on the purchase channel.

If you are looking at a specific model, would you like to check if it qualifies for Nissan's extended warranty programs (ワイド保証) in Japan, or do you need details on transferring an existing warranty to a second owner?

serpapi_response.reconstructed_markdown:
In Japan, Nissan's factory warranty explicitly separates coverage periods into General Warranty \(一般保証\) and Special Warranty \(特別保証\), with specific, extended terms assigned to electric vehicles \(EVs\) and e\-POWER hybrid models. [2] [6]

### Powertrain Coverage

Nissan categories powertrain components under its Special Warranty \(特別保証\). [6] [11] [12]

- Coverage Period: 5 years or 100,000 kilometers, whichever occurs first.
- Covered Components: The engine, transmission, transaxle, drivetrain, and related electronic control modules.
- e\-POWER Models: For e\-POWER hybrid vehicles \(like the Note or Serena\), the e\-POWER system components—including the generator and electric motor—are fully protected under this 5\-year/100,000 km powertrain specification.

### High\-Voltage EV Battery Coverage

Pure electric vehicles \(EVs\) like the Nissan Leaf or Ariya feature dedicated factory protection covering both manufacturing defects and capacity degradation: [2] [11] [1] [15] [16]

| Coverage Type | Period & Limit | Protection Details |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Defects Warranty | 8 years or 160,000 km | Covers repairs or replacements due to physical materials or factory workmanship flaws. |
| Capacity Warranty | 8 years or 160,000 km | Guarantees the lithium\-ion battery will not drop below 9 segments \(out of 12\) on the in\-vehicle segment gauge. Nissan repairs or replaces the pack if it falls below this minimum threshold. |
[2] [1] [17] [18]

Note: For e\-POWER vehicles, the smaller high\-voltage traction battery is typically backed by the standard 5\-year or 100,000 km special hybrid\-component breakdown criteria. [9] [10]

### Auxiliary 12V Battery

The standard lead\-acid auxiliary 12V battery used to boot vehicle electronics is excluded from the main EV and special powertrain plans. It is handled separately via individual battery manufacturer policies or standard 1\-to\-3 year general maintenance schedules based on the purchase channel. [16] [4] [7] [8] [19]

If you are looking at a specific model, would you like to check if it qualifies for Nissan's extended warranty programs \(ワイド保証\) in Japan, or do you need details on transferring an existing warranty to a second owner? [3]
